heading: Duties of chief judge
label: 253 Duties of chief judge
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 296 (, as added , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 187(a)Oct. 10, 1940, ch. 843, § 154 Stat. 1101This section contains a part of , U.S.C., 1940 ed. Other provisions of such section are incorporated in sections 251, 252, 254, 456, 1581, 2071, 2639, and 2640 of this title.section 296 of title 28Provision respecting recommendations for appointment, promotions, or otherwise affecting such clerical force, was omitted as unnecessary in view of .section 871 of this titleThe second paragraph is partly new and conforms with similar provisions of , relating to the chief judges of district courts.section 136(e) of this titleThe term “chief judge” was substituted for “presiding judge.” (See Reviser’s Note under .)section 136 of this titleChanges were made in phraseology and arrangement.

Amendments1996— struck out “; precedence of judges” after “chief judge” in section catchline and struck out subsecs. (d) and (e) which read as follows:Pub. L. 104–317“(d) Whenever the chief judge is unable to perform the duties of his office or the office is vacant, his powers and duties shall devolve upon the judge next in precedence who is able to act, until such disability is removed or another chief judge is appointed and duly qualified.“(e) The chief judge shall have precedence and shall preside at any session which he attends. Other judges shall have precedence and shall preside according to the seniority of their commissions. Judges whose commissions bear the same date shall have precedence according to seniority in age.”1980—Subsec. (a). redesignated the Customs Court as the Court of International Trade.Pub. L. 96–4171970— reorganized existing provisions into lettered subsecs. (a) to (e) and made minor changes in phraseology.Pub. L. 91–2711959— required the chief judge to supervise the fiscal affairs and clerical force of the court, with the approval of the court.Pub. L. 86–243

Effective Date of 1980 AmendmentAmendment by effective , and applicable with respect to civil actions pending on or commenced on or after such date, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 96–417section 701(a) of Pub. L. 96–417section 251 of this titleNov. 1, 1980
Effective Date of 1970 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 91–271section 122 of Pub. L. 91–271section 256 of this titleOct. 1, 1970

Savings ProvisionAmendment by not to deprive Customs Court [now Court of International Trade] officers or employees of any rights, privileges, or civil service status, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 86–243section 4 of Pub. L. 86–243section 871 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: The chief judge of the Court of International Trade, with the approval of the court, shall supervise the fiscal affairs and clerical force of the court;1So in original. The semicolon probably should be a period.1

children.label: b
children.text: The chief judge shall promulgate dockets.

children.label: c
children.text: The chief judge, under rules of the court, may designate any judge or judges of the court to try any case and, when the circumstances so warrant, reassign the case to another judge or judges.
